title: tigridia pugana
intro: Tigridia pugana, also known as the Mexican shellflower or Mexican daffodil, is a flowering plant native to Mexico and parts of Central America, characterized by its vibrant orange flowers and strap-like leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are believed to aid in wound healing and pain relief. The health benefits of Tigridia pugana include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. In herbal preparations, the plant's rhizomes and roots are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
